questions.question: What number comes before 50?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: The number before means 1 less.
Step 2: 50 − 1 = 49.
The number before 50 is 49.
Common mistake: Choosing 51 (the number after) or 40 (going back 10 instead of 1).

questions.question: What is 8 + 7?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: 8 + 7.
Step 2: We can split 7 into 2 and 5: 8 + 2 = 10, then 10 + 5 = 15.
Step 3: 8 + 7 = 15.
Common mistake: Choosing 14 (miscounting by 1) or 16 (adding an extra 1).

questions.question: How many corners does a square have?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: A square has 4 straight sides.
Step 2: Each place where two sides meet is a corner.
Step 3: A square has 4 corners.
Common mistake: Choosing 3 (confusing with a triangle).

questions.question: What is half of 16?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: Half means dividing into 2 equal parts.
Step 2: 16 ÷ 2 = 8.
Half of 16 is 8.
Common mistake: Choosing 4 (finding a quarter instead of a half).

questions.question: There are 2 groups of 5 oranges. How many oranges altogether?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: 2 groups of 5.
Step 2: 5 + 5 = 10.
Step 3: Or 2 × 5 = 10.
There are 10 oranges altogether.
Common mistake: Choosing 7 (adding 2 + 5) or 25 (writing 2 and 5 together).

questions.question: How many days are in one week?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: The days of the week are Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday.
Step 2: Count them: that is 7 days.
Common mistake: Choosing 5 (counting only school days).

questions.question: Lucy had 20 grapes. She ate 9. How many grapes are left?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: Lucy starts with 20 grapes.
Step 2: She eats 9: 20 − 9 = 11.
Lucy has 11 grapes left.
Common mistake: Choosing 29 (adding instead of subtracting) or 13 (subtracting 7 instead of 9).

questions.question: Which is heavier: 2 kg or 500 g?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: Convert to the same unit. 2 kg = 2000 g.
Step 2: Compare: 2000 g is more than 500 g.
Step 3: 2 kg is heavier.
Common mistake: Choosing 500 g because 500 looks like a bigger number than 2.

questions.question: Count by 10s: 10, 20, 30, ___. What comes next?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: We are counting by 10s.
Step 2: 30 + 10 = 40.
The next number is 40.
Common mistake: Choosing 31 (counting by 1 instead of 10).

questions.question: Which shape has no straight sides?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: A triangle has 3 straight sides. A rectangle has 4. A square has 4.
Step 2: A circle has a curved edge and no straight sides.
The circle has no straight sides.
Common mistake: Thinking all shapes must have straight sides.

questions.question: What is 56 − 29?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: 56 − 29.
Step 2: We can think: 56 − 30 = 26, then add back 1: 26 + 1 = 27.
Step 3: Or use column subtraction with borrowing.
56 − 29 = 27.
Common mistake: Choosing 37 (subtracting 19 instead of 29) or 33 (errors in borrowing).

questions.question: A packet has 10 biscuits. Mum buys 3 packets. How many biscuits are there?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: Each packet has 10 biscuits. There are 3 packets.
Step 2: 3 × 10 = 30.
There are 30 biscuits.
Common mistake: Choosing 13 (adding 10 + 3 instead of multiplying).

questions.question: What is a quarter of 20?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: A quarter means dividing into 4 equal parts.
Step 2: 20 ÷ 4 = 5.
A quarter of 20 is 5.
Common mistake: Choosing 10 (finding half instead of a quarter) or 4 (confusing with the number of parts).

questions.question: Tom measures his desk. It is 80 cm wide. How many centimetres short of 1 metre is his desk?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: 1 metre = 100 cm.
Step 2: The desk is 80 cm.
Step 3: 100 − 80 = 20 cm.
The desk is 20 cm short of 1 metre.
Common mistake: Choosing 80 cm (not understanding the question) or 10 cm (guessing).

questions.question: What is the biggest number you can make with the digits 3, 7 and 1?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: To make the biggest number, put the largest digit first.
Step 2: Arrange from largest to smallest: 7, 3, 1.
Step 3: The biggest number is 731.
Common mistake: Choosing 371 (not putting the largest digit first).

questions.question: Ella watches a show that starts at 4:30 and finishes at 5:00. How long is the show?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: The show starts at 4:30 and ends at 5:00.
Step 2: From 4:30 to 5:00 is 30 minutes.
The show is 30 minutes long.
Common mistake: Choosing 1 hour (thinking any gap to the next hour is 1 hour).

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a rectangle?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: A rectangle has 4 sides and 4 right angles.
Step 2: A square is a special type of rectangle. A door and a book cover are rectangular shapes.
Step 3: A triangle has 3 sides, so it is NOT a rectangle.
Common mistake: Choosing 'a square' (not knowing a square is a special rectangle).

questions.question: If you skip count by 2s starting from 3, which number will you say?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: Start at 3 and count by 2s: 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13, ...
Step 2: Check which option appears: 11 is in the list.
11 is a number you will say.
Common mistake: Choosing 8 or 10 (counting by 2s from 0 or 2 instead of from 3).

questions.question: Ryan has 34 cards. His friend gives him some more, and now he has 51. How many cards did his friend give him?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: Ryan had 34 and now has 51.
Step 2: The difference tells us how many he got: 51 − 34 = 17.
His friend gave him 17 cards.
Common mistake: Choosing 85 (adding 51 + 34) or 23 (subtracting incorrectly).

questions.question: There are 6 plates with 3 sandwiches on each. How many sandwiches are there?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: 6 plates with 3 sandwiches each.
Step 2: 6 × 3 = 18.
Step 3: Or add: 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 = 18.
There are 18 sandwiches.
Common mistake: Choosing 9 (adding 6 + 3) or 12 (calculating 4 × 3).

questions.question: A shop sells pencils for 45 cents each. How much do 2 pencils cost?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: Each pencil costs 45 cents.
Step 2: 2 pencils cost 45 + 45 = 90 cents.
Two pencils cost 90 cents.
Common mistake: Choosing 47 (adding 45 + 2) or 80 cents (adding 40 + 40, forgetting the 5s).

questions.question: What is the missing number? 15 + ___ = 42
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: We need to find what number added to 15 gives 42.
Step 2: 42 − 15 = 27.
Step 3: Check: 15 + 27 = 42. Correct!
The missing number is 27.
Common mistake: Choosing 57 (adding 15 + 42) or 33 (subtracting incorrectly).

questions.question: Jasper has 3 boxes. Each box has 5 toy cars. He gives 4 toy cars to his sister. How many toy cars does Jasper have left?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: 3 boxes × 5 cars = 15 toy cars.
Step 2: He gives away 4: 15 − 4 = 11.
Jasper has 11 toy cars left.
Common mistake: Choosing 4 (only subtracting: 8 − 4) or 8 (calculating 3 + 5 instead of 3 × 5).

questions.question: Mia's rope is 95 cm. She cuts off a piece and has 58 cm left. How long is the piece she cut off?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: The rope was 95 cm. After cutting, 58 cm is left.
Step 2: The piece cut off = 95 − 58 = 37 cm.
Step 3: Check: 58 + 37 = 95. Correct!
Common mistake: Choosing 43 (subtracting incorrectly) or 47 (errors in borrowing).

questions.question: How many quarters make a whole?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: A quarter is one of 4 equal parts.
Step 2: So 4 quarters make a whole.
Step 3: 1/4 + 1/4 + 1/4 + 1/4 = 4/4 = 1 whole.
Common mistake: Choosing 2 (confusing quarters with halves).

questions.question: What is double 35?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: Double means multiply by 2, or add the number to itself.
Step 2: 35 + 35.
Step 3: 30 + 30 = 60, and 5 + 5 = 10. Then 60 + 10 = 70.
Double 35 is 70.
Common mistake: Choosing 65 (adding 30 + 35) or 55 (doubling only part of the number).

questions.question: A clock shows 11:45. How many minutes until 12 o'clock?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: The time is 11:45.
Step 2: 12:00 − 11:45 = 15 minutes.
Step 3: From 45 minutes to 60 minutes (the full hour) is 15 minutes.
There are 15 minutes until 12 o'clock.
Common mistake: Choosing 45 minutes (reading the current minutes instead of calculating the remaining time).

questions.question: A hexagon has 6 sides. If you draw 3 hexagons, how many sides are there altogether?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: Each hexagon has 6 sides.
Step 2: 3 hexagons: 3 × 6 = 18 sides.
There are 18 sides altogether.
Common mistake: Choosing 9 (adding 6 + 3) or 12 (calculating 2 × 6).

questions.question: Liam has some coins: two 20-cent coins and three 10-cent coins. How much money does he have?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: Two 20-cent coins: 20 + 20 = 40 cents.
Step 2: Three 10-cent coins: 10 + 10 + 10 = 30 cents.
Step 3: Total: 40 + 30 = 70 cents.
Liam has 70 cents.
Common mistake: Choosing 50 cents (only counting one 20c and three 10c) or 60 cents (miscounting).

questions.question: There are 48 children in a hall. Half of them are boys. A quarter of the boys are wearing hats. How many boys are wearing hats?
questions.workedSolution:
Step 1: Half of 48 children are boys: 48 ÷ 2 = 24 boys.
Step 2: A quarter of the boys wear hats: 24 ÷ 4 = 6.
Step 3: 6 boys are wearing hats.
Common mistake: Choosing 12 (finding a quarter of 48 instead of a quarter of the boys) or 8 (miscalculating).
